course_description.overview: Unsupported ultracycling road bike challenge starting and finishing in Magdeburg, heading south into Harz National Park, zigzagging through the highest peaks and passes, then returning via the plains of Magdeburger Börde with additional climbs.

key_segments.name: Harz National Park
key_segments.description: Zigzag from east to west, conquering the highest paved peaks and passes in the Harz mountains.

key_segments.name: Magdeburger Börde Plains
key_segments.description: Exit Harz at Bad Harzburg, ride across vast plains back to Magdeburg with remaining climbs.

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Epic paved passes through mystical Harz landscapes demand endurance with 7,300m climbing over 555km in 48 hours; accessible entry-level ultra for solo or pairs on road bikes.

logistics.getting_there: Fly or train to Magdeburg; start/finish in Magdeburg.
logistics.accommodation: Self-arranged; unsupported event, plan overnight stays as needed within 48 hours.
logistics.registration_details: Solo: 199 € | Pair: 398 €; register via official site peaksandplains.cc or race result.
logistics.bike_logistics: Road bike recommended; fully unsupported, carry all supplies.
logistics.local_considerations: Harz National Park rules apply; weather variable with potential wind/fog on peaks; terms and conditions on official site.

final_verdict.one_liner: Unsupported road ultra through Harz peaks offers epic climbing and scenery at entry-level ultra price.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Solo or pair road cyclists seeking first ultra with big elevation in Germany's mystical Harz without gravel technicality.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for value-driven adventurers wanting unsupported challenge in stunning national park.
pro_tips: Train for sustained climbing on paved passes.
Start with full supplies as fully unsupported.
Check Harz weather for fog/wind on peaks.
Pair up to share load and motivation.
